info.title: Public API
info.description:
Access to the API resources is secured. To interact with the endpoints, the client must be authenticated using an `API Key`. This key must be included in the request headers of every API call to successfully verify your identity and authorize access.

API Key Authentication (System Flow): An API Key is very easy to use. Simply include your valid API key in the custom request header `x-api-key: <apiKeyValue>`. This method bypasses the login and token refresh requirements. To obtain an API key, log in to the portal and navigate to your profile page. 

Some endpoints contain `premium content` and require a paid plan or have different limits for free users. Depending on the plan, the user has different `monthly credits and rate limits`. For more information, see the [API pricing table](https://altfins.com/crypto-market-and-analytical-data-api/#pricing).

post.description: This endpoint fetches a collection of the trading signals generated by the altfins platform within a specified time frame. It is designed to help users quickly spot new trade opportunities as they appear, eliminating the need to manually scan the entire market. The output can be filtered by specific assets, signal types, and signal direction (BULLISH/BEARISH) within a defined time range.

400.description: Bad Request - Bean validation, business validation or parameter binding failed, validations field might by populated

post.description:
Filter and discover crypto assets using the same powerful logic as the [altFINS platform screener](https://altfins.com/crypto-screener). 

            Retrieve a filtered list of assets based on custom criteria such as price performance, volume, market capitalization, or technical indicator values.

400.description: Bad Request - Bean validation, business validation or parameter binding failed, validations field might by populated

post.description: This endpoint retrieves current market data for specified symbols. Users can define specific symbols in the request body. If the symbols array is empty, the endpoint returns a snapshot for all available symbols. Data is returned based on the selected time interval.

400.description: Bad Request - Bean validation, business validation or parameter binding failed, validations field might by populated

post.description: Retrieve historical OHLC time-series data for a specific asset within a defined date range and interval.

400.description: Bad Request - Bean validation, business validation or parameter binding failed, validations field might by populated

post.description: This endpoint allows you to query pre-processed summaries of market news articles from various sources. The returned data consists of condensed content where natural language processing has been applied to extract key information from long-form text. This allows users to quickly grasp market sentiment and identify major events without reading full articles.

400.description: Bad Request - Bean validation, business validation or parameter binding failed, validations field might by populated

post.description: This endpoint allows you to fetch a single news summary. It requires a specific combination of MessageId and SourceId to return the requested record. This is typically used to display the full detail view of an item selected from the general news list.

400.description: Bad Request - Bean validation, business validation or parameter binding failed, validations field might by populated

get.description:
Access curated, expert-led technical analysis covering 50+ major cryptocurrencies. 

These are trade setups including:
* Entry zones
* Exit targets
* Stop-loss levels
* Clear technical reasoning
* Designed for traders who want structured decision support, not noise.

post.description:
Returns jwt access and refresh tokens.
Access token is used to access other API endpoints and has short expiration time.
Refresh token is used to get a fresh pair of access and refresh tokens and has a long expiration duration.
Refresh token can be used only once.

symbols.description: Chosen symbol names or empty if snapshot for all symbols should be loaded

items.description: Chosen symbol names or empty if snapshot for all symbols should be loaded

signals.description: Chosen signal keys or empty if all signals should be loaded

items.description: Chosen signal keys or empty if all signals should be loaded

AnalyticsComparisonsFilter.description: You can include multiple filter objects in the request body to compare two analytics metrics against each other. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

analyticsComparisonsFilterType.description: Filter type for comparing two analytics

CandlestickPatternFilter.description: You can include multiple candlestick pattern filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

candlestickPatternFilterType.description: Type of candlestic pattern to filter

candlestickLookBackIntervals.description: Filter the results by type of coin. Only coins matching the specified type will be returned.

CrossAnalyticFilter.description: You can include multiple cross analytic filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

crossAnalyticFilterType.description: Type of cross analytic to filter

crossAnalyticFilterValue.description: Filter results by an exact cross analytic direction.

crossLookBackIntervals.description: Number of past intervals to check for a cross. For example, 3 means look for cross events in the last 3 intervals. Default is 5.

NumericFilter.description: You can include multiple numeric filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

numericFilterType.description: The numeric market data type used to filter the result.

gteFilter.description: Greater than or equal filter. When provided, only items with a numeric field value ≥ the given value are returned.If the result should be only within the defined range, fill in both fields 'gteFilter' and 'lteFilter'

lteFilter.description: Less than or equal filter. When provided, only items with a numeric field value ≤ the given value are returned. If the result should be only within the defined range, fill in both fields 'gteFilter' and 'lteFilter'

symbols.description: Chosen symbol names or empty if snapshot for all symbols should be loaded

items.description: Chosen symbol names or empty if snapshot for all symbols should be loaded

timeInterval.description:
The time interval for market data calculation. 
 
| Value | Description | 
| :--- | :--- | 
| `MINUTES15` | 15-minute intervals | 
| `HOURLY` | 1-hour intervals | 
| `HOURS4` | 4-hour intervals |

displayType.description: A list of screener market data types whose values will be returned in the response. If parameter is empty, only symbol, name and last price values will be returned in the response

items.description: A list of screener market data types whose values will be returned in the response. If parameter is empty, only symbol, name and last price values will be returned in the response

numericFilters.description: You can include multiple numeric filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

signalFilters.description: You can include multiple signal filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

crossAnalyticFilters.description: You can include multiple cross analytic filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

candlestickPatternFilters.description: You can include multiple candlestick pattern filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

analyticsComparisonsFilters.description: You can include multiple filter objects in the request body to compare two analytics metrics against each other. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

coinTypeFilter.description: Filter the results by type of coin. Only coins matching the specified type will be returned.

coinCategoryFilter.description: Filter by specific market segments. Items must belong to **each** selected category.

items.description: Filter by specific market segments. Items must belong to **each** selected category.

tradingTypeFilter.description: Filter the results by trading type. When multiple trading types are specified, all must match for a coin to be included in the response.

items.description: Filter the results by trading type. When multiple trading types are specified, all must match for a coin to be included in the response.

exchangeFilter.description: A list of exchange identifiers used to filter results. When provided together with `tradingTypeFilter`, only coins that are tradable in **all selected trading types** and are **listed on all specified exchanges** will be returned. If `tradingTypeFilter` is not defined, the default is `SPOT`.

items.description: A list of exchange identifiers used to filter results. When provided together with `tradingTypeFilter`, only coins that are tradable in **all selected trading types** and are **listed on all specified exchanges** will be returned. If `tradingTypeFilter` is not defined, the default is `SPOT`.

athDateBeforeFilter.description: Filters results to include only items whose ATH (All-Time High) date is on or before the specified timestamp. The value must be an ISO-8601 date-time string

athDateAfterFilter.description: Filters results to include only items whose ATH (All-Time High) date is on or after the specified timestamp. The value must be an ISO-8601 date-time string.

percentDownFromAthFilter.description:
Filters assets based on the distance between the **Last Price** and the **All-Time High (ATH)**.  

| Category | Value | Logic |  
| :--- | :--- | :--- |  
| **Within Range** | `PRICE_AT_LEAST_X_PERCENT_BELOW_ATH` | Price is **within** X% of the ATH (Price $>=$ ATH - X%). |  
| **Farther Than** | `PRICE_AT_LEAST_X_PERCENT_MORE_ATH` | Price is **more than** X% below the ATH (Price $=<$ ATH - X%). |  
 
#### Available Thresholds:  
* **Below (Within):** 1%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 30%  
* **More (Farther):** 1%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 20%, 30%  
 
**Example Use Case:**  
* Use `PRICE_AT_LEAST_5_PERCENT_BELOW_ATH` to find coins currently `testing` or hovering near their record highs.  
* Use `PRICE_AT_LEAST_30_PERCENT_MORE_ATH` to find coins that have significantly retraced from their peak.

supportResistanceFilter.description:
The parameter allows to filter results based on the relationship of price action to predefined support and resistance levels. 
| Value | Description | 
| :--- | :--- | 
| `APPROACHING_SUPPORT` | Price is nearing a support level from above. | 
| `BROKEN_BELOW_SUPPORT` | Price has closed or moved significantly below support. | 
| `APPROACHING_RESISTANCE` | Price is nearing a resistance level from below. | 
| `BROKEN_ABOVE_RESISTANCE` | Price has closed or moved significantly above resistance. | 
 
 **Note:** Use this in conjunction with `supportResistanceLookBackIntervals` to define the search window.

supportResistanceLookBackIntervals.description: Number of past intervals to check for a broken below support and broken above resistance level. For example, 3 means look for levels in the last 3 intervals. Default is 5.

weekAnalytics52Filter.description: Filter criteria for 52-week price analytics. When provided, this field restricts results based on comparisons between a security’s last price and its 52-week high/low values.

rsiDivergenceFilter.description:
Filter results by the type of RSI (Relative Strength Index) divergence detected in the analytics data. 
RSI divergence occurs when price action and the RSI indicator move in opposing patterns, often signaling shifts in momentum that may indicate trend reversals or continuations.

newLowInLastPeriodFilter.description: Filter to return results where a new low was recorded within the last N periods.

newHighInLastPeriodFilter.description: Filter to return results where a new high was recorded within the last N periods.

macdFilter.description:
Filters assets based on the **MACD Line** relationship with its **Signal Line**. This is a primary indicator for identifying trend momentum shifts. 
 
| Signal | Technical Condition | Market Sentiment | 
| :--- | :--- | :--- | 
| `BUY` | **MACD Line > Signal Line** | **Bullish momentum**: Often occurs during an upward crossover or when price is accelerating north. | 
| `SELL` | **MACD Line < Signal Line** | **Bearish momentum**: Often occurs during a downward crossover or when price is accelerating south. |

macdHistogramFilter.description:
Filter results based on the direction of the MACD histogram value.
The MACD Histogram measures the distance between the MACD Line and the Signal Line (Histogram = MACD - Signal). This filter identifies the direction and strength of market momentum. 
 
| Value | Direction | Technical State | Momentum Interpretation | 
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | 
| `H1_UP` | **Positive / Rising** | Histogram > 0 and Increasing | **Strong Bullish**: Upward momentum is accelerating. | 
| `H1_DOWN` | **Positive / Falling** | Histogram > 0 and Decreasing | **Weakening Bullish**: Price is still up, but momentum is fading. | 
| `H2_DOWN` | **Negative / Falling** | Histogram < 0 and Decreasing | **Strong Bearish**: Downward momentum is accelerating. | 
| `H2_UP` | **Negative / Rising** | Histogram < 0 and Increasing | **Weakening Bearish**: Price is still down, but selling pressure is fading. |

minimumMarketCapValue.description: Optional minimum market cap value filter

SignalFilter.description: You can include multiple signal filter objects in the request body. Multiple filters use **AND** logic

signalFilterType.description: The signal market data type used to filter the result.

signalFilterValue.description: Filter results by an exact signal value.

symbols.description: Chosen symbol names or empty if snapshot for all symbols should be loaded

timeInterval.description: Time interval of snapshot, defaults to DAILY

time.description: Timestamp in ISO date time format

from.description: Lower bound for time in ISO-8601 instant format. Results will have time >= from

to.description: Upper bound for time in ISO-8601 instant format. Results will have time <= to

timeInterval.description: Time interval of analytics, defaults to DAILY

from.description: Lower bound for time in ISO-8601 instant format. Results will have time >= from

to.description: Upper bound for time in ISO-8601 instant format. Results will have time <= to

time.description: Timestamp in ISO date time format

nonNumericalValue.description: Non numerical analytics value, one of NUMERICAL, BUY, STRONG_BUY, SELL, STRONG_SELL, NEUTRAL, BEARISH, HIDDEN_BEARISH, BULLISH, HIDDEN_BULLISH, OVERBOUGHT, OVERSOLD, PEAK, TROUGH, UP, DOWN, STRONG, VERY_STRONG, WEAK, VERY_WEAK, TRUE, FALSE, BEARISH_AND_HIDDEN_BULLISH, BULLISH_AND_HIDDEN_BEARISH, VERY_OVERSOLD, VERY_OVERBOUGHT, ABOVE, BELOW

isNumerical.description: If true then the analytic has non numerical values

accessToken.description: Short lived jwt token to access other API endpoints

refreshToken.description: Long lived jwt token used to get a new pair of tokens when access token expires, can be used only once
